parent_statement: Energy and energy efficiency actions under the Climate Plan (priority actions list).
coverage_assessment: The sub-actions collectively align with the parent goal of stationary energy actions by covering demand-side efficiency (new-build standards, savings in existing buildings) and supply-side measures (renewable generation), plus public lighting efficiency. A key gap is the absence of specificity on which building segments are targeted (public/private, residential/commercial) and how savings/standards will be achieved.
coherence_assessment: The actions are complementary (codes for new buildings, efficiency in existing buildings, efficient public lighting, and renewable supply) with no contradictions. There is no sequencing, governance, or dependencies described (e.g., who adopts/enforces the code, how projects are funded and rolled out).

initiative_reasoning: 1) The initiative aims to prioritize stationary energy actions in Dakar via efficiency (codes and savings), public lighting upgrades, and renewable electricity expansion. 2) Sub-action strength mix: 0 strong, 2 moderate (building code; solar LED public lighting), 2 weak (generic energy savings; generic renewable capacity strengthening). 3) The parent provides a city-level scope (City of Dakar / stationary energy sector) but does not supply a binding mechanism (no ordinance/mandate) or any measurable targets/timelines that could lift weaker items. 4) Coverage is reasonably broad across demand and supply (coverage_score 0.65) and the list is internally coherent (coherence_score 0.75), but measurability is absent at both parent and sub-action levels (aggregate_measurability = No). 5) Applying the INITIATIVE SOUNDNESS RULE: it fails SOUND (no aggregate measurability; no strong sub-actions) and fails PARTIAL (no measurable target or deadline anywhere in the cluster). Therefore the initiative is WEAK and final_verdict is False.

parent_statement: Solid waste and wastewater actions under the Climate Plan (priority actions list).
coverage_assessment: The two sub-actions align with the parent header by addressing solid waste (organics recovery) and wastewater-related pollution (sludge management) as priority actions. However, the scope is narrow and high-level: it does not specify broader solid waste system elements (collection coverage, landfill methane, recycling) or wastewater treatment performance, so coverage of an overall solid waste/wastewater goal is only partial.
coherence_assessment: The sub-actions are complementary: one targets upstream diversion/recovery of organics while the other targets downstream sludge handling to reduce pollution load. There are no contradictions, but there is also no sequencing, responsible entity, or linkage (e.g., how recovery channels will be developed and operated).
weak_signals: no numeric targets, no timelines/deadlines, no stated responsible actor, broad terms ('development', 'proper management')
strong_signals: Concrete intervention areas identified (organic waste recovery; sludge management in unserved areas)
initiative_reasoning: This initiative aims to list priority actions for the waste sector, focusing on organic waste recovery and improved sludge management from self-contained sanitation in unserved areas. The parent header provides neither an enforceable mechanism (e.g., ordinance/mandate) nor a clear inherited spatial scope beyond the phrase 'unserved areas' in Action 2, so there is little inherited context to strengthen the subs. Coverage is moderate (0.55) because the two actions do relate to solid waste and wastewater pollution, but leave major system components and performance targets unspecified. Coherence is reasonably high (0.75) because the actions are compatible and non-contradictory, though not presented as a delivery chain. Applying the Initiative Soundness Rule: aggregate measurability is 'No' and there are no strong/moderate sub-actions, so the cluster cannot be SOUND or PARTIAL; it is therefore WEAK.
